text:
F6 Words in rule 34.1(4) substituted (22.4.2014) by The Family Procedure (Amendment No.3) Rules 2013 (S.I. 2013/3204) , rules 1 , 105 (with rule 137 ); S.I. 2014/954 , art. 2


F7 Rules 34.1(5)(a)-(d) omitted (31.12.2020) by virtue of The Family Procedure Rules 2010 and Court of Protection Rules 2017 (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019 (S.I. 2019/517) , regs. 1(1) , 19(2)(b) (with reg. 27 ) (as amended by S.I. 2020/1574 , regs. 1 , 4(2) and S.I. 2020/1493 , regs. 1(1) , 8(2)(f) ); 2020 c. 1 , Sch. 5 para. 1(1)


F8 Rule 34.1(5)(e) and word substituted for full stop (20.12.2012) by The Family Procedure (Amendment No. 4) Rules 2012 (S.I. 2012/2806) , rules 1 , 18(b)


### Meaning of prescribed officer in [ F9 the family court ] E+W


34.2. —(1) For the purposes of the 1920 Act, the prescribed officer in relation to [ F10 the family court is the court officer ] .


(2) For the purposes of Part 1 of the 1972 Act F11 ..., the prescribed officer in relation to [ F12 the family court is the court officer ] .


[ F13 (3) For the purposes of an application under F14 ... Article 23(2) or (3) of the 2007 Hague Convention for registration of a maintenance order, the prescribed officer in relation to the family court is the court officer. ]


Textual Amendments

metadata.title: Part 34 — Reciprocal Enforcement of Maintenance Orders
